rationale: Civic discourse requires academic language; WIDA ELD standard 2 addresses language for social studies contexts.
Understanding rights and responsibilities maps to self-management and social awareness goals.
Demonstrating content knowledge of constitutional law directly reflects Danielson 1a: Knowledge of Content.
Comprehension of complex informational texts (founding documents) aligns with ICLP comprehension component.
REVIEW NEEDED – equity lens of constitutional history varies widely; alignment depends on instructional framing.
Language of argumentation and process description aligns with WIDA social studies language functions.
Understanding civic processes supports responsible decision-making (SEL goal 2A).
Teachers must understand democratic processes to design authentic learning (Danielson 1b).
Civil rights history is foundational to equity journey continuum work.
Recognizing others' rights and perspectives maps directly to social awareness standards.
Civil rights vocabulary is essential academic language targeted by ICLP vocabulary component.
Evaluating evidence requires language functions WIDA categorizes under argumentation.
Comprehension strategies (inference, synthesis) are explicitly targeted in ICLP Section 2.
Questioning and discussion techniques (Danielson 3b) are the instructional vehicle for civic analysis.
Cross-perspective dialogue is the operational definition of SEL social awareness in action.
Structured discussion is core to Danielson 3b: Questioning and Discussion Techniques.
Oral deliberation requires the academic oral language functions described in WIDA standard 1.
Oracy (oral language) is the first ICLP component; deliberation is its civic application.
Evaluating digital and print sources maps directly to ICLP comprehension strategies.
Interpreting media requires the language of analysis and evaluation (WIDA ELD standard 3).
Media literacy supports self-awareness and critical consumption (SEL 1A).
Recognizing bias in media is central to equity journey continuum work on systemic awareness.
Selecting appropriate digital resources reflects Danielson 1c: Setting Instructional Outcomes.
Explicit, sequenced civic instruction directly corresponds to Danielson 3c: Engaging Students in Learning.
Direct instruction in word recognition and fluency is explicitly named in ICLP components 3-4.
Structured language instruction for ELL students aligns with WIDA ELD standard 4.
REVIEW NEEDED – direct instruction alignment with SEL responsible decision-making is indirect.
Writing for inquiry (ICLP writing component) directly mirrors the research and inquiry process.
Designing inquiry-based learning reflects Danielson 1e: Designing Coherent Instruction.
Academic research requires language functions in WIDA standard 3 (interpretive mode).
Student agency is the operational outcome of SEL self-management standards.
Student voice is a primary indicator in EJC equity domain 4 (shared power).
Student engagement and agency map to Danielson 3d: Using Assessment in Instruction.
Community partnerships are explicit in EJC domain 4 (belonging and community).
Relationship skills with community organizations directly maps to SEL standard 2C.
Service learning involves communicating with families and community (Danielson 4c).
Service learning is the civic application of responsible decision-making and prosocial behavior (SEL 3B).
Reciprocal service with community reflects EJC's community co-creation principle.
Service learning projects require persuasive and expository writing targeted by ICLP component 7.
Community engagement contexts provide authentic oral language use aligned with WIDA standard 2.
Belonging and inclusive culture is the foundational domain (1) of the Equity Journey Continuum.
Inclusive culture operationalizes SEL social awareness at the school-systems level.
Creating an environment of respect and rapport is Danielson domain 2a.
Using disaggregated data for equity action is central to EJC domain 3 (equitable systems).
ICLP Section 5 explicitly requires equity-centered data analysis for literacy improvement.
Growing and developing professionally includes equity-focused professional learning (Danielson 4e).
WIDA emphasizes drawing on students' home language assets; culturally responsive teaching is essential.
Culturally responsive practice is explicitly named in EJC domain 2 (identity and culture).
ICLP requires contextualization of literacy instruction to students' cultural and linguistic backgrounds.
